concerns.text: The plan assumes the HCP 1200 release contains a 'sleep.csv' file with items to construct a 'Sleep Score' proxy. The HCP 1200 behavioral data does not contain a dedicated sleep quality index (like PSQI) or a single 'sleep.csv' file; sleep-related items are scattered across the 'Questionnaire' data (e.g., 'Sleep' section in the main behavioral CSV or specific questionnaire files). Relying on a non-existent 'sleep.csv' will cause the ingestion pipeline to fail immediately. The plan must specify the exact behavioral file(s) and column names (e.g., 'Sleep_Duration', 'Sleep_Quality' from the HCP Questionnaire data) to be used, or acknowledge that the outcome variable derivation is more complex than stated.

The plan targets GitHub Actions free-tier runners (≤6 GB RAM, ≤5 hours) to process ~200-300 subjects with 1,000 permutations of a nested 5x5 CV loop. Even with dimensionality reduction, running 25,000 full model fits (1000 perms * 5 outer * 5 inner) on a 2-vCPU, 7GB RAM runner within 5 hours is highly infeasible for fMRI connectivity data. The computational cost of re-running the entire preprocessing and feature extraction pipeline for every permutation (as required by FR-006) on this hardware is likely to exceed the time limit by an order of magnitude. The plan needs a revised compute strategy (e.g., pre-computed null distribution, reduced permutations, or external compute) to be feasible.
